faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: Check-in from 3:00 PM, check-out by 10:00 AM. Luggage storage on request.
faq.q2: How does the offline deposit work?
faq.a2: Present yourself to the receptionist with your Credit Card (no Debit Card) upon arrival. You will sign a form that will be destroyed within 8 hours of your departure. No charge or hold on the card.
faq.q3: How does the online deposit (Stripe) work?
faq.a3: Stripe pre-authorizes the amount on your card without charging it. At checkout the amount is released automatically if no damages.
faq.q4: What if self check-in is rejected?
faq.a4: The rejection message explains the reason. Message the host or contact us via WhatsApp.
faq.a6: Yes, you can request a camping cot with bedding included at a cost of €40 per stay. Book it at least 72 hours in advance by contacting the host via WhatsApp at +39 328 313 1500.

depositoClient.title: Security deposit
depositoClient.subtitle: How it works, how much it is, and when it is refunded.
depositoClient.whyText: The security deposit protects the property against accidental damage during your stay. It is not an extra charge: it is refunded in full within 48 hours of departure if there is no damage. You can pay it in two ways: in person on arrival with a credit card, or online in advance via Stripe.
offlineSteps: Present yourself to reception with your Credit Card (no Debit Card, no PostePay).
The receptionist will fill in an authorisation form which you will sign.
The form is kept until your departure and then destroyed.
No pre-authorisation on your card: it is a paper guarantee only.
depositoClient.offlineNote: You can change your mind and switch to the online payment method at any time from your Guest Area.
onlineSteps: Log in to your Guest Area with your booking number and email.
Select "Security deposit" and choose the online method.
Stripe places a hold on your card — no actual charge is made.
You receive immediate confirmation by email.
depositoClient.onlineNote: Your card details are never shared with us. Stripe is PCI DSS Level 1 certified — the same standard used by banks.
depositoClient.amountsNote: The exact amount is shown on each apartment listing. As a guide:
returnSteps: Staff carry out a property inspection within 2 hours of check-out.
If there is no damage, the deposit is refunded within 48 hours of departure.
For the online method (Stripe), the pre-authorisation expires automatically in 7–30 days depending on the card network (Visa, Mastercard, Amex).
If there is damage, you will receive photographic documentation and a cost estimate before any deduction is made.
depositoClient.damagesTitle: What happens if there is damage?
depositoClient.damagesText: If damage is found, we will contact you within 24 hours of departure with photographic documentation and a cost breakdown. You will be able to dispute or agree on the amount before any deduction is made. Any deduction is always proportionate to the actual damage and never exceeds the deposit amount.

faqs.q: Can I pay with a prepaid card or PostePay?
faqs.a: No. A real Credit Card is required (Visa, Mastercard, Amex). Debit cards and prepaid cards are not accepted for either the offline or online method.

faqs.q: Is the deposit charged immediately with the online method?
faqs.a: No. Stripe places a pre-authorisation: the amount is "held" on your card but not charged. If there is no damage, the hold expires automatically with no charge.

faqs.q: What if I forget to pay the deposit before arrival?
faqs.a: No problem. You can always pay in person on arrival with your credit card. We recommend you bring it with you.

faqs.q: Is the deposit the same for every apartment?
faqs.a: No. The amount varies based on the size and type of property. You will find it on the listing for the apartment you have booked.

Yes. Before any deduction we will send you photographic documentation and a cost breakdown. You can dispute the amount and agree on a solution. If there is a disagreement, your bank's chargeback procedure or PayPal's dispute process are available.

depositoClient.ctaTitle: Ready to pay the deposit online?
depositoClient.ctaText: Log in to your Guest Area with your booking number and email to proceed.
depositoClient.ctaNote: You will need your booking number — it is in your confirmation email.

contatti.pageSubtitle: Find your answer below, or write to us directly.
contatti.safeBannerText: Want to know who we are before booking?

items.q: Do children up to 2 years old pay?
items.a: Children up to 2 years old are not included in the price but are counted towards the maximum capacity of the property and do not pay tourist tax.

items.q: Is a camping cot available?
items.a: Yes, for children up to 2 years old a camping cot with highchair is available on request at €40 per stay. Please book at least 72 hours in advance by contacting us via WhatsApp.

items.q: Do children aged 3 to 11 pay?
items.a: Children aged 3 to 11 pay the same rate as adults and do not pay tourist tax.

items.q: What are the check-in and check-out times?
items.a: Check-in from 4:00 PM to 7:00 PM, check-out from 8:00 AM by 10:00 AM.

items.q: Can I arrive after 7:00 PM?
items.a: Yes. If your flight does not allow you to arrive by 7:00 PM, or if you prefer to be free from reception hours, you can request self check-in.

link.label: Find out how self check-in works →

items.q: Can I check out before 8:00 AM?
items.a: Yes. If you need to leave before 8:00 AM, let us know when you arrive and we will arrange an early check-out together.

items.q: Can I request an early check-in?
items.a:
In almost all cases the previous guest leaves at 10:00 AM and we use the following six hours to prepare everything for your arrival.
In exceptional cases it may be possible: contact us a few days before arrival to check availability.

items.q: How does key handover work?
items.a: We are personally present to welcome you upon arrival.

items.q: How does self check-in work?
items.a:
Self check-in is not automatic: it must be requested at least 72 hours in advance.
• Request self check-in at least 72 hours before arrival
• Send ID documents of all adult guests (legible and complete)
• You must be reachable in the 2 hours before arrival

items.q: How do I get here by train? Is the station nearby?
items.a:
Minturno-Scauri station is in the centre of town.
Journey times: approximately 1h40 from Rome, 60 minutes from Naples.
Visit trenitalia.com for timetables and tickets.

items.q: Where is the reception for physical check-in?
items.a: You will receive detailed check-in information within 7 days before your arrival.

items.q: Is there a WhatsApp number for direct contact?
items.a: Yes, you can contact us via WhatsApp at the number shown on this page. We use it to organise arrival, check-in and any needs during your stay.

items.q: Is the pool open? What time of year?
items.a: The pool is generally available from 30 May to 15 October. Exact dates depend on the property and weather conditions. Contact us to confirm for your specific dates.

items.q: Is the pool private (exclusive use) or shared?
items.a: The type of pool, where available, is indicated in the property description (shared or exclusive).

items.q: Is the pool included in the price?
items.a: Yes, use of the pool is fully included in the rental price. There are no additional costs.

items.q: Is the pool suitable for young children? Is it safe?
items.a: Children may only use the pool when accompanied by an adult. The main pool has a maximum depth of 1.20 metres.

items.q: The photos show different pools — which one is included?
items.a: The type of pool (shared or exclusive), where available, is indicated in the property features.

items.q: Is the accommodation entirely ours? Are there no other guests?
items.a: Yes, each accommodation unit is reserved exclusively for the guests who book it. Spaces such as the car park, pool and sun terrace area may be shared with guests from other units within the same complex.

items.q: Are bed linen and towels included?
items.a: Yes, bed linen and bathroom towels are included in the price. Beach towels are not provided.

items.q: Is the final cleaning mandatory? How much does it cost?
items.a: Yes, the final cleaning is included in the price unless otherwise stated during booking. Cleaning does not include daily room tidying.

items.q: Are energy costs (electricity, gas, water) included in the price?
items.a:
No. Energy costs are charged separately, measured by meter readings at check-in and check-out. You only pay for what you actually consume.
In winter, the cost depends heavily on how much you use the heating.

items.q: Is WiFi available? Is it fast enough for remote working and video calls?
items.a: Yes, WiFi is available in all LivingApple properties. The connection is sufficient for remote working and professional video calls, though performance varies between properties. Contact us for the exact speed.

items.q: Is heating guaranteed in winter?
items.a:
Yes, all properties are equipped with a working heating system. In the event of technical problems, we are available promptly.
Please note: energy costs (electricity, gas, water) are measured by meter and charged separately at the end of your stay.

items.q: Is there private parking? Is it free?
items.a: Yes, each property has private parking on site or nearby. In some cases it may be chargeable: this is indicated on the property listing.

items.q: Is it easy to park in Scauri?
items.a: Yes, parking is generally not a problem. At the property itself you can purchase a weekly blue-zone permit for €15.00, valid in Scauri and the entire Municipality of Minturno.

items.q: How do I deal with a technical problem during my stay?
items.a: In case of technical problems (hot water, heating, etc.) contact us immediately on WhatsApp. For urgent issues, our response priority is maximum and we intervene as quickly as possible.

items.q: Can I get a towel change or cleaning during my stay?
items.a:
Yes, during your stay you can request:
• Towel change: €4 per set per person
• Mid-stay cleaning: cost and date to be agreed at check-in
For stays of more than one week, we recommend arranging this at arrival.

items.q: How far is the sea? Are there equipped beaches?
items.a: LivingApple countryside is about 2-3 km from Scauri/Minturno beach. LivingApple sea is just 250 metres away. There are equipped beach clubs reachable by car or on foot.

items.q: Is the property in Scauri or Minturno? How do I find it?
items.a: LivingApple properties are located between Minturno and Scauri (LT), in southern Lazio. We will send you the exact address and Google Maps pin in the welcome message.

items.q: Are there supermarkets, restaurants and shops nearby?
items.a: Yes, in Scauri and Minturno you will find supermarkets, bars, restaurants, pharmacies and shops a few minutes away by car. Travel times vary depending on the property chosen.

items.q: How far is it from Rome Fiumicino Airport?
items.a: Rome Fiumicino Airport (FCO) is approximately 165 km away, reachable in about 1h40-2h00 by car (A1 motorway). Alternatively: train from Roma Termini to Minturno-Scauri station.

items.q: How long does it take from Rome and Naples by car or train?
items.a:
By car: from Rome approx. 1h40 (A1 motorway), from Naples approx. 1h (A1).
By train: from Roma Termini approx. 1h40, from Napoli Centrale approx. 60 minutes.
Minturno is halfway between Rome and Naples.

items.q: Is the road to the property difficult?
items.a: The main access roads are easy to navigate. For hillside properties we will send you detailed directions and the recommended route in the welcome message.

items.q: Is the tourist tax included in the price?
items.a:
Yes, it is included in the price shown on our website.
OTA platforms (Airbnb and Booking) have difficulty adapting the calculation for each individual Municipality: in Minturno the tax is €2 per night per adult (maximum 10 nights). Children under 12 are exempt.

items.q: How does the cancellation policy work?
items.a: It depends on the rate chosen at the time of booking. Full details are shown on the offer page.

items.q: Is a security deposit required?
items.a:
Yes, a security deposit will be required on arrival. The exact amount varies by property and is shown on our website.
A credit card will be required on arrival (not Debit Card, not PostePay). The deposit will be fully refunded within 48 hours of departure if there is no damage.

items.q: Can the price be negotiated for long stays or groups?
items.a: For stays of one month or more, a personalised rate can be arranged. Contact us directly with the exact dates and number of guests.

items.q: I have seen different prices on different pages — which one is correct?
items.a: The correct and final price is the one shown on the listing detail page at the time of booking. The price never includes energy consumption.

items.q: Is it possible to get an invoice for business stays?
items.a: Yes, invoices can be issued for business stays. Contact us before booking, providing your company name, VAT number and stay dates.

items.q: Are energy costs (electricity, gas, water) included in the price?
items.a:
No. Energy costs are charged separately, measured by meter readings at check-in and check-out. You only pay for what you actually consume.
In winter, the cost depends heavily on how much you use the heating.

ui.hero_title: Scauri, like you've never experienced it
ui.hero_sub: Book directly with us and save on commissions

utenzeClient.pageSubtitle: This page exists because we want your holiday to have no unpleasant surprises.
utenzeClient.disclaimer: In accordance with EU Directives 2011/83/EU and 2019/2161/EU, we inform you that supplementary charges will apply for water, electricity and gas consumption, measured by meter readings at check-in and check-out.
utenzeClient.meterNote: Meters are read at arrival and departure. You are charged only for what you actually consume. Prices may be updated and any changes will be communicated before booking confirmation.
utenzeClient.ratesNote: Prices shown may be subject to change. Any updates will be communicated before booking confirmation.
utenzeClient.wIntroText: Southern Italian buildings were designed to resist summer heat, not to retain winter warmth. Breathable walls, high ceilings, natural ventilation: excellent qualities in summer, but in winter they make any heating system highly inefficient. Energy demand is 2.5 times higher than in modern buildings.
utenzeClient.wAcc1Title: LPG vs city gas — why it costs more
utenzeClient.wAcc1Text: Our area is not connected to the natural gas pipeline network. We use LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas), delivered by lorry and stored in an on-site tank. LPG costs 30–50% more than city gas per unit of heat produced, due to transport and logistics costs. This is not our choice — it is the reality of the territory and applies to all properties in the area.
utenzeClient.wAcc2Title: The condensation problem — please read carefully
utenzeClient.wAcc2Text:
Approximately 50% of our winter guests leave the property with condensation problems. The cause is almost always the same: windows kept closed at all times, heating on full, no ventilation.

In Northern Europe, ventilation is handled automatically by mechanical systems. In our property, ventilation is natural: windows must be opened regularly.

The solution is simple: open windows fully for 5–10 minutes, 2–3 times a day. Bedroom in the morning, bathroom after showering, kitchen after cooking. The heating recovers the temperature within a few minutes. Failure to ventilate, if it causes mould damage, may result in a deduction from the security deposit.
utenzeClient.wAcc3Text:
• Set heating to 19–20°C, not 23–25°C
• Switch off when leaving for more than 30 minutes
• Keep doors to unused rooms closed
• Avoid drying clothes indoors
• Open windows for 5–10 minutes, 2–3 times a day
utenzeClient.sIntroText: Air conditioning is available in all LivingApple properties. Using it correctly is a matter of health before it is a matter of cost. The WHO recommends a maximum difference of 7°C between indoor and outdoor temperature. Italian public health regulations (DPR 74/2013) state that air conditioning should not be set below 24°C. Italian research published in Clinical and Translational Allergy: respiratory damage begins below 24°C.
utenzeClient.sAcc1Text:
Going from 35°C outside to 18°C inside (a 17°C gap) is not cooler in a safe way — it is a chronic thermal shock. Documented risks: sore throat, cough, summer bronchitis, neck pain, muscle cramps, gastrointestinal problems. These symptoms can ruin a holiday.

Recommended indoor temperature in summer: 24–26°C. At 26°C with dehumidified air you feel cool and comfortable. Each degree lower on the thermostat increases energy consumption by approximately 6–8%.
utenzeClient.sAcc2Title: Windows open with the AC on
utenzeClient.sAcc2Text:
In Northern Europe, you may be used to keeping windows slightly open while heating or cooling runs. In our property, this immediately cancels out all the cooling effect and forces the unit to run at maximum capacity continuously.

Always keep windows and doors closed whenever the air conditioning is on.
utenzeClient.sAcc3Text:
• Set AC to 24–26°C — cool and comfortable
• Switch it off when you leave: there is no benefit to cooling an empty room
• Use the dehumidifier mode (DRY) if available: much lower energy consumption
• Keep shutters and blinds closed during the hottest part of the day
• Never open windows while the AC is running
utenzeClient.honestText: If you prefer not to think about energy use during your holiday — and we completely understand — we honestly suggest looking for a property with all-inclusive pricing. It is a perfectly valid choice. Not a compromise: simply the right tool for your needs.
utenzeClient.honestCaution:
Warning: some all-inclusive properties at very low prices have heating and cooling systems inadequate for Northern European standards, consumption caps above which a surcharge applies, or insufficient electrical supply — circuit breakers that trip and leave you without power.

Before booking, always ask:
• "Does the property reach 22°C indoors in January?"
• "Is there a consumption cap on the included utilities?"
• "Is the electrical supply sufficient for all air conditioners running simultaneously, plus oven, hairdryer, washing machine, dishwasher and microwave?"
